description: a4/ workspace hooks. Most logic lives in the Python dispatcher `scripts/a4_hook.py` (subcommands: pre-edit, post-edit, stop, user-prompt). Three small bash hooks (`cleanup-edited-a4.sh`, `sweep-old-edited-a4.sh`, `refresh-rule-symlinks.sh`) handle trivial tmp-file lifecycle and post-upgrade rule-symlink refresh. Session-scoped state under `.claude/tmp/a4-edited/`: `a4-edited-<sid>.txt` (paths edited this session, consumed by Stop), `a4-resolved-ids-<sid>.txt` (issue ids already announced via UserPromptSubmit, dedupes per-session), and `a4-prestatus-<sid>.json` (pre-edit `status:` snapshot keyed by absolute path, populated by PreToolUse and consumed by PostToolUse to detect transitions). PreToolUse stashes the on-disk `status:` of the file about to be edited. PostToolUse records the edit, runs the status-change cascade (supersedes / discarded / revising flips on related files) when pre→post is a legal transition, refreshes `updated:` on the primary, then reports per-component status-consistency on the post-cascade state. Stop runs frontmatter schema checks AND a transition-legality safety net (HEAD-vs-working-tree git diff against `FAMILY_TRANSITIONS`) on session-edited files — catches illegal direct `status:` edits that the cascade hook intentionally ignored. UserPromptSubmit resolves `#<id>` references in the prompt to `a4/<type>/<id>-<slug>.md` paths and injects them as `additionalContext` so Claude reads the file directly instead of searching. SessionStart no longer reports workspace-wide status-consistency — run `/a4:validate` (or invoke `validate.py` directly) for a manual sweep. Design principles — language choice, lifecycle symmetry, ordering, non-blocking, output channels — live in `dev/hook-conventions.md`. For edits that bypassed the cascade hook (manual git checkout, external editors), run `/a4:validate --fix` (i.e. `validate.py <a4-dir> --fix`) — it walks live successors and reconciles `superseded` flips. Cascade primitives shared by the hook and the recovery sweep live in `scripts/status_cascade.py`.

hooks.description: Snapshot on-disk a4/*.md `status:` so post-edit can detect transitions precisely
Record edited a4/*.md, run status-change cascade, then report per-component status-consistency
Validate a4/*.md edited this session; exit 2 on violations to force fix
Delete this session's a4 record files
Sweep orphan a4 record files older than 1 day
Repoint stale <project>/.claude/rules/ symlinks at the current ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} (no-op if /a4:install-rules was never run)
Resolve #<id> references in the prompt to a4/<type>/<id>-<slug>.md paths (per-session dedupe)
